posts.author: PrismShapes
posts.content: just spent an hour arguing with my friend that modern ufc fighters would kill a gladiator in hand to hand combat. he said gladiators fought to kill while ufc fighters play by the rules. it literally drove me out of my mind. has anyone else had a similar conversation?

posts.author: The Duck
posts.content: A gladiator would also be like 4 foot 2 because we as an human race has gotten bigger over time

posts.author: B7L7
posts.content:
@PrismShapes never talked about fighters vs gladiators but spent a lot of time talking with a couple of people who don't train at all thinking they can beat me in a street setting.I think most casual, untrained people think fighters "training to play by the rules" will mean anything in a street fight. If it's a street fight the rules automatically cease to exist. I'm kicking you in the groin, gouging your eyes, breaking your nose with my forehead.... More often than not it's an ego thing, men hate when someone is more athletic and stronger than them. It's natural to want to compete. But untrained people who think they can hang with professional fighters in a street fight without a weapon are out of their minds. If you think a fighter will not be able to read your weak ass punch or a slow ass kick, you're fooling yourself.

* Edited at 04.02.2026,  2:39 AM ET *

posts.author: B7L7
posts.content:
Since that is clearly a serious conversation and a debate between you and your friend, let's just set things straight. The average height of a gladator was 5'6"-5'7". Majority of them had some form of hand-to-hand combat training which mostly consisted of wrestling and boxing, but their primary focus was armed combat, meaning most of them could indulge in simple unarmed combat centered around raw aggression. Unarmed combat was not their speciality, and was used as a last resort if the lost their weapon. Today's MMA training is super specialised. Modern fighter should win this fight.

* Edited at 04.02.2026,  3:22 AM ET *

posts.author: JM Barrie
posts.content:
Listen, century after century, generations get softer and softer.
Gladiators were around starting around 4th century BC.
Alexander the Great was conquering the Persian Empire.
Socrates was executed via posion.
Sparta lost the Battle of Leuctra.
Think of going to battle with swords.
Dying by swords.
Over 700,000 people died in the Battle of Fei River.
The Chinese went to war with these BTW

Yall can talk all you want about height and weight.  Yall can talk about hand to hand combat skills all you want.

The 21st century could never match the brutality of the 4th century gladiator.

You think a gladiator cares about TTD? You think they care about BJJ?  You think they care about Boxing?
A gladiator would kill you with a sword, a mace, a spear.  They'd chop your head off without thinking twice.
How is a UFC fighter going to kill gladiator? MMA skills would be worthless in 4th century gladiator combat.

posts.author: B7L7
posts.content:
@JM Barrie as far as I'm aware we were talking about even field, no weapons allowed. Even a crackhead with a gun or a knife has the upper hand against a combat sport athlete.

* Edited at 04.02.2026,  6:32 AM ET *
